title: Cephaelis glabrescens
intro: Cephaelis glabrescens, also known as the Ecuadorian or Colombian uncaria, is a plant species in the Rubiaceae family, native to the tropical regions of South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its health benefits, including its potential to treat fever, headaches, and as a diuretic, due to its antimalarial, anti-inflammatory, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Cephaelis glabrescens include alkaloids such as uncarine and uncarine-A, which have been shown to exhibit significant antimalarial activity. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, and is commonly used to make herbal supplements and extracts, as well as being incorporated into various traditional medicine formulas.
taxonomy: Cephaelis glabrescens is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This plant is a member of the Rubiaceae family, commonly known as the coffee or madder family. The Rubiaceae family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants, which also includes other species such as coffee and gardenia. Cephaelis glabrescens is further classified within the genus Cephaelis, which is a group of plants native to the Americas. The classification of Cephaelis glabrescens is a reflection of its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within the Rubiaceae family.
uses: Cephaelis glabrescens, a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for various purposes. The plant's bark, leaves, and roots are used to treat different ailments, including fever, diarrhea, and respiratory issues. In some regions, it is believed to possess antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help in wound healing and reducing swelling. However, there is limited scientific research available on the plant's medicinal properties, and more studies are needed to confirm its effectiveness. Further research is necessary to fully understand the potential benefits and risks associated with using Cephaelis glabrescens for medicinal purposes.

constituents: Cephaelis glabrescens is a plant species that has been used in traditional medicine, and its active constituents are primarily alkaloids. preparations: Cephaelis glabrescens, also known as the uncinatum tree, is used in traditional herbal preparations.
